(Downtown, lit. "Center") is a neighborhood in the [Central Region](/wiki/Zona_Central,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Zona Central, Rio de Janeiro") of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"). It represents the financial heart of the city, and the crux of the Central Region.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

**Centro** (Downtown, lit. "Center") is a neighborhood in the [Central Region](/wiki/Zona_Central,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Zona Central, Rio de Janeiro") of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"). It represents the financial heart of the city, and the crux of the Central Region.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Characteristics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Centro,_Rio_de_Janeiro&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Characteristics")]

Despite still having a large number of residences, the neighborhood is predominantly commercial with a mixture of historical buildings as well as modern skyscrapers. Residential areas lie mainly along Rua do Riachuelo and Castelo. The historic and financial centre of the city,[[2]](#cite_note-2) sites of interest include the [Paço Imperial](/wiki/Pa%C3%A7o_Imperial "Paço Imperial"), [Candelária Church](/wiki/Candel%C3%A1ria_Church "Candelária Church"), [Igreja de Santa Rita de Cássia](/wiki/Igreja_de_Santa_Rita_de_C%C3%A1ssia_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "Igreja de Santa Rita de Cássia (Rio de Janeiro)"), the [Old Cathedral of Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Old_Cathedral_of_Rio_de_Janeiro "Old Cathedral of Rio de Janeiro"), and the modern-style [Saint Sebastian's Cathedral](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro_Cathedral "Rio de Janeiro Cathedral"). Around [Marechal Floriano Square](/wiki/Cinel%C3%A2ndia "Cinelândia"), there are several landmarks from the *[Belle Époque](/wiki/Belle_%C3%89poque "Belle Époque")* such as the [Municipal Theatre](/wiki/Teatro_Municipal_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "Teatro Municipal (Rio de Janeiro)") and the National Library building. The Centro area also has several museums such as the [National Museum of Fine Arts](/wiki/Museu_Nacional_de_Belas_Artes "Museu Nacional de Belas Artes") and the [National Historical Museum](/wiki/National_Historical_Museum_(Brazil) "National Historical Museum (Brazil)").

Other important historical attractions in downtown Rio include its [Passeio Público](/wiki/Passeio_P%C3%BAblico_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "Passeio Público (Rio de Janeiro)"), an 18th-century public garden, as well as the imposing arches of the [Carioca Aqueduct](/wiki/Carioca_Aqueduct "Carioca Aqueduct") and the [Casa Cavé](/wiki/Casa_Cav%C3%A9 "Casa Cavé"), the oldest confectionery in the city. A "bondinho" (tram) leaves from a station near Saint Sebastian's Cathedral, crosses the aqueduct (converted to a tram viaduct in 1896) and rambles through the hilly streets of the nearby [Santa Tereza](/wiki/Santa_Teresa_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "Santa Teresa (Rio de Janeiro)") neighbourhood. Downtown remains the heart of the city's business community. Some of the largest companies in Brazil have their head offices here, including [Petrobras](/wiki/Petrobras "Petrobras"), [Eletrobras](/wiki/Eletrobras "Eletrobras"), [BNDES](/wiki/BNDES "BNDES") and [Vale](/wiki/Vale_(mining_company) "Vale (mining company)"). Most of Rio's skyscrapers, especially the tallest ones, are located in this neighbourhood. Crowded from Monday to Friday during regular work hours, it becomes empty during the evening and on Saturdays, Sundays, and holidays.
